description: Research drug-gene directionality (inhibitor vs activator) for 12 actionable keloid candidates via ChEMBL API with DrugBank/PubMed fallback. Produces per-drug research docs and a combined annotations JSON consumed by scripts/08_import_directionality.py.

userStories.title: Initialize directionality output files
userStories.description: As the research pipeline, I need an empty annotations JSON and output directory before per-drug stories run.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory `docs/directionality/` if it does not exist
Create `data/directionality_annotations.json` containing `[]` (valid empty JSON array) if it does not exist
If the file already exists with content, leave it alone (idempotent)
`python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('data/directionality_annotations.json'))"` succeeds

userStories.title: Research LENALIDOMIDE directionality
userStories.description: Determine action_direction for LENALIDOMIDE against TNF, VEGFA, KDR, CTNNB1. Follow research protocol in docs/ralph-prd-directionality.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Query ChEMBL mechanism endpoint for LENALIDOMIDE (pref_name lookup → molecule_chembl_id → mechanism.json)
For each of TNF, VEGFA, KDR, CTNNB1: set action_direction (inhibitor/activator/modulator/unknown) using ChEMBL first, DrugBank + PubMed fallback if unknown
Write `docs/directionality/LENALIDOMIDE.md` with drug class, ChEMBL ID, gene-by-gene table, 2-3 sentence summary, sources
Append 4 entries to `data/directionality_annotations.json` (one per target gene)
Each entry has all 8 fields: drug_name, gene_symbol, action_direction, confidence, source, keloid_target_role, net_effect, notes
TNF entry has net_effect='unclear' regardless of direction (context_dependent target role)
Final `data/directionality_annotations.json` is valid JSON (verified with `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('data/directionality_annotations.json'))"`)

userStories.title: Research RITUXIMAB directionality
userStories.description: Determine action_direction for RITUXIMAB against IL6, JAK2, TGFB1. Biologic — expect ChEMBL miss on our gene list, fall back to DrugBank/PubMed.
acceptanceCriteria: Attempt ChEMBL lookup for RITUXIMAB (document the result — primary target is CD20/MS4A1 which is not in our gene list, so expect no matches for IL6/JAK2/TGFB1)

For IL6, JAK2, TGFB1: determine action_direction from DrugBank mechanism page + PubMed abstracts (rituximab is an anti-CD20 antibody; any effect on IL6/JAK2/TGFB1 is indirect — mark as 'unknown' with low confidence if no clear literature)
Write `docs/directionality/RITUXIMAB.md` with drug class, ChEMBL ID (or 'not found'), gene-by-gene table, summary explaining indirect mechanism, sources
Append 3 entries (one per target gene) to `data/directionality_annotations.json`
Each entry has all 8 fields with keloid_target_role='pro_keloid' for all three
`data/directionality_annotations.json` remains valid JSON after append

userStories.title: Research IMATINIB directionality
userStories.description: Determine action_direction for IMATINIB against EGFR, JAK2, SMAD3, CTNNB1.
acceptanceCriteria: Query ChEMBL mechanism endpoint for IMATINIB
For each of EGFR, JAK2, SMAD3, CTNNB1: set action_direction using ChEMBL → DrugBank → PubMed fallback chain
Write `docs/directionality/IMATINIB.md` with drug class (tyrosine kinase inhibitor), ChEMBL ID, gene-by-gene table, summary, sources
Append 4 entries to `data/directionality_annotations.json`
Each entry has all 8 fields with keloid_target_role='pro_keloid'
Annotations JSON remains valid

userStories.title: Research CELECOXIB directionality

acceptanceCriteria: Query ChEMBL mechanism endpoint for CELECOXIB
For each of STAT3, PIK3CA, VEGFA, CTNNB1: set action_direction via ChEMBL → DrugBank → PubMed fallback (document indirect mechanisms where applicable)
Write `docs/directionality/CELECOXIB.md` with drug class (COX-2 inhibitor/NSAID), ChEMBL ID, gene-by-gene table, summary, sources
Append 4 entries to `data/directionality_annotations.json`
Each entry has all 8 fields with keloid_target_role='pro_keloid'
Annotations JSON remains valid

userStories.title: Research PEGINTERFERON ALFA-2B directionality
userStories.description: Determine action_direction for PEGINTERFERON ALFA-2B against EGFR, IL6, JAK2. Biologic — likely ChEMBL miss, rely on literature.
acceptanceCriteria: Attempt ChEMBL lookup for PEGINTERFERON ALFA-2B (primary target is IFNAR1/IFNAR2 interferon receptors; not in our list)
For EGFR, IL6, JAK2: determine action_direction from DrugBank + PubMed (type I interferon signals through JAK/STAT — JAK2 activation is a known downstream effect; document clearly)
Write `docs/directionality/PEGINTERFERON_ALFA-2B.md` with drug class, ChEMBL ID or 'not found', gene-by-gene table, summary, sources
Append 3 entries to `data/directionality_annotations.json`
Each entry has all 8 fields with keloid_target_role='pro_keloid'
Annotations JSON remains valid

userStories.title: Validate complete annotations JSON
userStories.description: Final pass: verify all 12 drugs have research docs and every drug-gene pair has a well-formed JSON entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Exactly 12 files exist in `docs/directionality/` (one per drug, matching the drug name uppercase with underscores)
`data/directionality_annotations.json` contains exactly 38 entries (4+3+4+4+3+4+3+4+3+2+2+2 = 38 drug-gene pairs from the PRD)
Every entry has all 8 required fields: drug_name, gene_symbol, action_direction, confidence, source, keloid_target_role, net_effect, notes
Every action_direction value is one of: inhibitor, activator, modulator, unknown
Every confidence value is one of: high, medium, low
Every net_effect value is one of: beneficial, unfavorable, unclear
All TNF entries have net_effect='unclear' (LENALIDOMIDE-TNF, THALIDOMIDE-TNF)
Write `docs/directionality/VALIDATION.md` summarizing counts, any 'unknown' pairs flagged, and overall completeness check
